statute_citation: 40 ILCS 5/4-110.1 (municipalities 500,000 and under); 40 ILCS 5/6-151.1 (cities over 500,000); 820 ILCS 305 (Workers' Compensation Act); 820 ILCS 310 (Workers' Occupational Diseases Act)
filing_deadline_notes: Presumption does not apply to employees with less than 5 years at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim with IL WCC (820 ILCS 305/310). Pension provisions require disease to develop/manifest during active service.
notes: Pension provisions (40 ILCS 5/4-110.1 and 6-151.1) require 5 years (Article 4) or 7 years (Article 6) of service, active status, and disease to manifest during service. Article 4 applies to municipalities 500K and under; Article 6 to cities over 500K (7-year minimum). WC/OD Acts (820 ILCS 305/310) require 5 years employment at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim. Pre-employment exam required under pension provisions: if exam shows impairment, firefighter loses entitlement until subsequent exam shows no impairment.

condition_specific: tuberculosis, disease of the lungs or respiratory tract
filing_deadline_notes: 5-year employment minimum at time of filing for WC/OD Acts; disease must manifest during active service for pension provisions.
notes: Tuberculosis and lung/respiratory disease covered under all four statutory provisions. Article 4 (5-year minimum), Article 6 (7-year minimum). Pre-employment exam required under pension provisions.

condition_specific: disabling cancer that may be caused by exposure to heat, radiation, or a known carcinogen as defined by IARC
filing_deadline_notes: Cancer must develop or manifest during period while firefighter is in service (pension provisions). 5-year minimum at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim (WC/OD Acts).
notes: Cancer type must be one that may be caused by exposure to heat, radiation, or a known IARC carcinogen (4-110.1 and WC/OD Acts). Pre-employment exam: if exam discloses existence of cancer, firefighter loses entitlement until subsequent exam shows no cancer. Article 6 (cities over 500K) requires 7 years.

condition_specific: AIDS, hepatitis C, bloodborne pathogens, MRSA (contagious staph infection)
filing_deadline_notes: Article 6 requires disease during active service. WC/OD Acts require 5 years at time of filing.
notes: AIDS and hepatitis C covered in Article 6 (cities over 500K; 7-year minimum) and WC/OD Acts. MRSA covered under all three WC/OD-related provisions. Pre-employment exam required under Article 6: if exam discloses AIDS, hepatitis C, or MRSA, firefighter loses entitlement until subsequent exam shows no impairment.

filing_deadline_notes: 5-year minimum at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim.
notes: Hernia and hearing loss explicitly included in the rebuttable presumption under both the Workers' Compensation Act and the Workers' Occupational Diseases Act.

condition_specific: heart or vascular disease or condition, hypertension
filing_deadline_notes: 5-year minimum at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim with IL WCC.
notes: WC/OD Acts cover EMT, EMT-I, A-EMT, and paramedic. Exclusion: presumption does NOT apply to EMT/A-EMT/paramedic employed by a private employer if the employee spends the preponderance of work time engaged in medical transfers between medical care facilities or non-emergency medical transfers.

condition_specific: lung or respiratory disease or condition, tuberculosis
filing_deadline_notes: 5-year minimum at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim.
notes: Covers EMT, EMT-I, A-EMT, and paramedic. Private-employer/medical-transfer exclusion applies.

condition_specific: cancer caused by exposure to heat, radiation, or IARC-defined carcinogen
filing_deadline_notes: 5-year minimum at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim.
notes: Covers EMT, EMT-I, A-EMT, and paramedic. Private-employer/medical-transfer exclusion applies.

condition_specific: bloodborne pathogens, MRSA (contagious staph infection), tuberculosis
filing_deadline_notes: 5-year minimum at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim.
notes: Covers EMT, EMT-I, A-EMT, and paramedic. Private-employer/medical-transfer exclusion applies.

filing_deadline_notes: 5-year minimum at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim.
notes: Hernia and hearing loss explicitly included for EMT, EMT-I, A-EMT, and paramedic. Private-employer/medical-transfer exclusion applies.

Section 4-112 provides special protections for firefighters receiving disability pensions for PTSD: mandatory annual medical re-examination may be discontinued if firefighter has reached age 45, has documentation from 2+ physicians approving discontinuance, and board votes affirmatively. Also, no physical or mental disability forming the basis of a pension application may be used as cause for discharge by municipalities or fire protection districts.
